title: Packaged device with additive substrate surface modification
abstract: In described examples, a method (100) of lead frame surface modification includes providing (101) at least one pre-fabricated metal lead frame or package substrate (substrate) unit including a base metal having a die pad and a plurality of contact regions surrounding the die pad. An ink including a material that is a solid or a precursor for a solid that forms a solid upon a curing step or a sintering step that removes a liquid carrier is additive ly deposited (102) including onto at least one of (i) a region of the die pad and (ii) at one region of at least a first of the contact regions (first contact region). The ink is sintered or cured (103) to remove the liquid carrier so that a substantially solid ink residue remains.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method of substrate surface modification, comprising: providing at least one pre-fabricated metal lead frame or package substrate (substrate) unit including a base metal having a die pad and a plurality of contact regions surrounding the die pad; additively depositing an ink including a material that is a solid or a precursor for a solid that forms a solid upon a curing step or a sintering step in a liquid carrier onto at least one of (i) a region of the die pad and (ii) at least one region of a first of the contact regions (first contact region), and sintering or curing the ink to remove the liquid carrier to form an ink residue. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the additively depositing includes inkjet printing using piezoelectric, thermal, acoustic, or electrostatic inkjet printing, screen printing, or flexographic printing. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the substrate unit includes a plurality of the metal lead frame units joined as a panel to one another. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the substrate unit includes a plurality of the package substrate units joined as a panel to one another. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the additively depositing then the sintering or the curing forms the ink residue in a pattern of islands which maintains at least one non-modified metal surface region on the die pad and at least one non-modified metal surface region on the first contact region. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the pattern of islands provides a 0.1 Î¼Î¹Î· to > 50 Î¼Î¹Î· root mean square (rms) surface roughness. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the material include nanoparticles of a metal material. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the metal material is different than the base metal. 9. The method of claim 5, further comprising electroplating over the pattern of islands with a precious metal layer or a precious metal alloy layer. 10. The method of claim 5, wherein the material include a dielectric material, and wherein the pattern of islands is a pattern of plating stop islands, further comprising: plating a metal layer between the pattern of plating stop islands, and removing the pattern of plating stop islands to 

 form a plurality of metal pillars. 11. The method of claim 1 , wherein the material include a material that has a surface energy less than a surface tension of a fluid material that is dispensed on the material. 12. The method of claim 10, wherein the dielectric material includes a polymer or an oxide material. 13. The method of claim 1 , further comprising adding a die attach material onto the die pad, attaching an integrated circuit (IC) device onto the die pad, and wire bonding to couple bond pads on the IC device to the contact regions. 14. A packaged semiconductor device, comprising: a metal lead frame or package substrate (substrate) unit including a base metal having a die pad and a plurality of contact regions surrounding the die pad; an additive adhesion layer including an ink residue or spaced apart metal pillars on at least one of (i) a region of the die pad and (ii) a first of the contact regions (first contact region); a die attach material on the die pad; an integrated circuit (IC) device on the die pad having a plurality of bond pads, and coupling from respective ones of the bond pads and respective ones of the contact regions. 15. The packaged semiconductor device of claim 14, further comprising bond wires, wherein the bond pads are coupled to the contact regions by the bond wires. 16. The packaged semiconductor device of claim 14, wherein the bond pads are coupled to the contact regions by a flip chip arrangement so that the packaged semiconductor device includes a flip chip package. 17. The packaged semiconductor device of claim 14, wherein the ink residue is arranged in a pattern of islands which maintains at least one non-modified metal surface region on the die pad and at least one non-modified metal surface region on the first contact region. 18. The packaged semiconductor device of claim 17, wherein the pattern of islands provides a 0.1 Î¼Î¹Î· to >50 Î¼Î¹Î· root mean square (rms) surface roughness. 19. The packaged semiconductor device of claim 17, further comprising a precious metal layer or a precious metal alloy layer over the pattern of islands. 20. The packaged semiconductor device of claim 17, wherein the ink residue includes a dielectric material.
